facts.definition: Routes selected after gas and all configured gates in the retained Base run.
facts.limitations: Zero selected routes is specific to this retained run; it is not a claim about all periods, execution success, or realized profit.
facts.workload: base-trace-20260709T144000Z; 500 blocks; 1,279,758 evaluated routes; retained simulation/replay artifact

facts.definition: Highest observed per-window route-evaluation rate in the retained run; distinct from aggregate sustained throughput.
facts.limitations: A peak window is not sustained performance and does not establish opportunity recall, execution success, realized profit, or commercial validation.
facts.workload: base-trace-20260709T144000Z; 500 blocks; 1,279,758 evaluated routes; retained simulation/replay artifact

facts.definition: Aggregate evaluated-route count divided by measured service time across the retained run; distinct from the highest per-window peak.
facts.limitations: Throughput does not establish opportunity recall, execution success, realized profit, current live performance, or commercial validation.
facts.workload: base-trace-20260709T144000Z; 500 blocks; 1,279,758 evaluated routes; retained simulation/replay artifact

facts.definition: Generated candidate routes with at most four hops in the retained Ethereum route catalog.

facts.definition: Tracked docs/walkthroughs Markdown corpus: 14 numbered walkthroughs plus INDEX.md and README.md.
facts.limitations: This is a defined walkthrough corpus, not every architecture or process document in the repository.

facts.definition: Cargo workspace packages returned by cargo metadata --no-deps --format-version 1 at the audited Salus commit.
facts.limitations: A package count measures workspace structure, not production maturity, commercial validation, or profitability.

work.display_label: Salus trading-system research and reference implementation
work.limitations: Not commercially validated or production-proven profitable; the latest cited retained runs selected zero after-gas routes.
work.relationship: An active Jincubator initiative led and implemented by John Whitton.
work.summary: Researches ingestion, graph construction, candidate-route generation, evaluation, and the boundary between simulated signals and executable outcomes.
